perturbation.original_text: All uses of Wade Image in connection with this Agreement shall be subject to the prior written approval of Wade, which Wade shall not unreasonably withhold. Naked agrees to submit to Wade or its authorized agent a copy of all new promotional and/or advertising material using Wade Image at least ten (10) days prior to the release to the general public. Wade and its authorized agent agree that they shall not unreasonably withhold such approval. In the absence of disapproval within three (3) business days of Wade's or authorized agent's receipt of a request for approval, said advertising or promotional material shall be deemed approved. Any such usage featuring Athlete shall be and remain the property of Naked except as otherwise set forth herein; however, Naked shall have the right to such use only during the Term. Wade may use said materials in whole or in part for Wade's personal portfolio, website or otherwise. Such usage may not be sold or transferred.
perturbation.changed_text: All uses of Wade Image in connection with this Agreement shall be subject to the prior written approval of Wade. Naked agrees to submit to Wade or its authorized agent a copy of all new promotional and/or advertising material using Wade Image prior to the release to the general public. Any such usage featuring Athlete shall be and remain the property of Naked except as otherwise set forth herein; however, Naked shall have the right to such use only during the Term. Wade may use said materials in whole or in part for Wade's personal portfolio, website or otherwise. Such usage may not be sold or transferred.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the clause 'which Wade shall not unreasonably withhold' and removing the details of the approval timeframe, it creates ambiguity about what constitutes a valid approval or rejection. The original clause emphasizes the requirement of reasonableness, clarifying the standard for Wade's approval, while the new clause provides Wade with an unqualified right to approve or disapprove material. Then due to this clause being omitted, it created an in-text contradiction on the right of Wade to fully reject promotional material, without reasonable cause, in contrast, if this isn't being followed. It contradicts with the text in section 6 where it states: 'Naked may also propose to use the Commercial Materials as part of cooperative advertising and retail tie in promotions subject to Wade's prior written approval which shall not be unreasonably withheld, provided that withholding approval because a potential tie in conflicts with one of Athlete's existing sponsors shall not be deemed unreasonable.'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Naked may also propose to use the Commercial Materials as part of cooperative advertising and retail tie in promotions subject to Wade's prior written approval which shall not be unreasonably withheld, provided that withholding approval because a potential tie in conflicts with one of Athlete's existing sponsors shall not be deemed unreasonable. Notwithstanding the foregoing, in no case shall the Commercial Materials feature any commercial tie in or other use that could be perceived as an endorsement by Athlete of any products or services other than the Naked Products. Naked shall comply with all applicable rules and regulations (including the NBA's) in its use of the Commercial Materials hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text included a clause stating that Wade's approval could not be unreasonably withheld. The changed text removes this clause, granting Wade unrestricted approval rights. This directly contradicts the text in section 6, which explicitly states that Wade's approval 'shall not be unreasonably withheld' for cooperative advertising and retail tie-in promotions. The omission creates a situation where Wade could unreasonably withhold approval for all uses of Wade Image per the changed text, conflicting with the explicitly limited circumstances in section 6 where Wade's approval cannot be unreasonably withheld, resulting in a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: If requested, Wade will pass out a reasonable number of samples of the Wade Product. Wade will also permit himself to be reasonably photographed at the Personal Appearances with customers and fans.
perturbation.changed_text: Wade will also permit himself to be photographed at the Personal Appearances with customers and fans.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the term 'reasonable' from the section 'Wade will also permit himself to be reasonably photographed at the Personal Appearances with customers and fans.', this now creates an omission in-text contradiction as Section 10 C states: 'Exercise reasonable constraints to avoid taking any actions which damages Naked, its name, reputation, image, the Naked Products and Wade Products;', in comparison to what is established.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Exercise reasonable constraints to avoid taking any actions which damages Naked, its name, reputation, image, the Naked Products and Wade Products;
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that Wade would be 'reasonably' photographed, aligning with Section 10 C's requirement to 'exercise reasonable constraints.' By removing 'reasonable' from the modified text, Wade is now obligated to be photographed without any constraints, which directly contradicts Section 10 C's requirement for reasonableness. This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency within the document.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the Agreement is terminated in accordance with Section 12A, than the [***] term of the Grant Warrant [***] shall expire ninety (90) days thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the Agreement is terminated in accordance with Section 12A, than the [***] term of the Grant Warrant [***] shall continue without restriction.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the section so that the 'term of the Grant Warrant will continue without restriction', creates an in-text contradiction to the section in 12A. where it states:'Such termination shall relieve Naked of its obligation to provide any further consideration pursuant to this Agreement provided that Wade shall retain all warrants he has received hereunder that have vested as of the date of such termination. In the event of such termination as a result of a material breach of this Agreement by Wade (i) Wade's contractual liabilities and obligations until the date of termination still exist notwithstanding such termination, (ii) Naked shall be under no obligation to sell any Wade Products but shall nevertheless owe the Royalty on any Wade Products sold; and (iii) notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, Wade shall forfeit all warrants he has received hereunder. Naked shall not have waived any of its rights at law or in equity by exercising any provision of this section.'. Where it is clearly stating that if Wade breaches that Warrant will be forfieted, in comparison to the modified text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Naked shall have the right to terminate this Agreement upon ten (10) days prior written notice to Wade in the event Wade fails to perform the Wade Services or breaches any other covenant or agreement set forth herein (including the essence of this Agreement), and fails to cure same (if curable) within seven (7) days of receipt of written notice. Such termination shall relieve Naked of its obligation to provide any further consideration pursuant to this Agreement provided that Wade shall retain all warrants he has received hereunder that have vested as of the date of such termination. In the event of such termination as a result of a material breach of this Agreement by Wade (i) Wade's contractual liabilities and obligations until the date of termination still exist notwithstanding such termination, (ii) Naked shall be under no obligation to sell any Wade Products but shall nevertheless owe the Royalty on any Wade Products sold; and (iii) notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, Wade shall forfeit all warrants he has received hereunder. Naked shall not have waived any of its rights at law or in equity by exercising any provision of this section.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Grant Warrant expires 90 days after termination under Section 12A. The changed text states that the Grant Warrant continues without restriction after termination under Section 12A. Section 12A states that Wade forfeits all warrants if the termination is due to Wade's material breach. The changed text directly contradicts Section 12A because it implies Wade's warrants continue 'without restriction' regardless of breach, conflicting with the forfeiture provision. This creates a clear contradiction.
